sample.title: How Data Centers in Space Could Change AI
sample.description:
“Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier.

How to Build a Data Center in Space: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn
Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos plan to make AI extraterrestrial.

Building data centers in space is still a relatively nascent idea, but it’s gaining traction and financial backing, as energy and space constraints limit the expansion of massive computing facilities here on Earth. Earlier this year, in the latest leg of their decades-long space race, Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin both announced plans to build and launch so-called orbital data centers.

Learn more: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn

01:37-03:43 Starcloud’s big ambition

sample.title: Dario Amodei Addresses Reports of Claude’s Role in US Military Strikes
sample.description:
On The Circuit with Emily Chang, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei addresses reports that the US military is using Claude as part of the US-Israel war with Iran, including during a strike in Iran that destroyed a girls' school, killing more than 150 people. 

Amodei concedes "terrible" mistakes will be made but defends the integration of AI in defense by emphasizing that "a human makes the final call."

sample.title: What The U.S.-Iran Deal Could Mean For Markets And Your Wallet
sample.description:
President Trump and Iranian leaders signed a memorandum of understanding that should reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lower oil prices and ease shipping costs. The deal could bring relief for consumers through cheaper gas, airfare and goods, but risks around regional security, inflation, Fed policy and interest rates remain.

sample.title: How dating became a financial luxury
sample.description:
Half of single Americans say they are going on fewer dates or choosing less expensive activities because of rising costs, according to BMO’s 2026 survey.

But, the cost of dating itself is only part of the story. For millions of users, finding a date means paying for the apps.

sample.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
sample.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

0:00-54:00 Introduction
00:55-03:16 Chapter 1: Computing Costs
03:17-05:27 Chapter 2: Demand
05:28-07:21 Chapter 3: Setting a Benchmark

sample.title: OPEC chief dismisses IEA supply glut forecast as 'critical' Strait of Hormuz reopens
sample.description:
OPEC chief Haitham al-Ghais sat down for an exclusive interview with CNBC on Wednesday.

He dismissed the IEA’s claim of a supply glut brewing in oil markets for 2027.

“What does the IEA see that OPEC and the rest don’t see?” he added.

sample.title: Vance warns Israeli critics over Iran deal: Trump is your ‘only powerful’ ally
sample.description:
U.S. Vice President JD Vance lashed out at Israeli critics of the Iran deal, saying President Donald Trump is Israel's only ally, in a sharp rebuke that referenced the billions in U.S. defense aid the country receives.

Vance ‌was defending the deal reached this week to end the war with Iran that critics in the U.S. and Israel have slammed for failing to curb Iran's missile program and providing no clear path to dismantling its nuclear facilities, while constraining Israel in its war with Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.

sample.title: Who won the US-Iran Deal? | BBC Newscast
sample.description:
Today, the US and Iran have an agreement which extends the ceasefire between the two countries, and is now signed by US President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.

Chief International Correspondent Lyse Doucet talks us through what the deal includes, what it means for the strait of Hormuz and oil prices, and where the sticking points may be. Chief North America Correspondent Gary O’Donoghue explains how the deal has been spun and how it has been received in the US.

And, we continue digging into the proposed social media ban with a special guest from Australia. Australian Liberal MP Zoe McKenzie had been calling for this ban since her maiden speech in 2022, she explains how it actually works and what it could take to change how the tech companies protect young people online.

00:25 Iran-US Deal Signing Expected in Switzerland
01:24 Trump Signs Agreement at Versailles as Leaders Respond
03:51 Ceasefire Plan and Questions Over a “Permanent” End to War
06:03 Strait of Hormuz Reopens as Markets and Oil Prices Shift
09:56 Reconstruction Fund and Sanctions Relief Under Scrutiny
14:59 What the Deal Leaves Out: Missiles and Proxies 
18:12 Australia’s Social Media Ban and Impact on Children
25:07 Can Platforms Enforce Age Limits and Close Loopholes?

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

sample.title: The UK is banning social media for children under the age of 16. Should the US do the same? #BBCNews
The vaccine preventing the deadly cancer, HPV | BBC News
sample.description:
Children vaccinated at age 12–13 against HPV (human papillomavirus) have close to zero risk of dying from cervical cancer before the age of 30, landmark new research has revealed.

The first study of its kind shows deaths have fallen sharply since school-age girls began being offered it in 2008, and around 200 lives have been saved in England so far thanks to the vaccine.

Between 2020 and 2024, no cervical cancer deaths were recorded in women aged 20 to 24 - the first time that had happened over a five-year period.

Without vaccination, around 23 deaths would have been expected.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews
